info.title: GENI API
info.description:
# GENI API

The GENI API is the primary interface for interacting with the GENI platform. API users can create, view, interact with, and modify GENI bots.
More information about the GENI platform's API can be found on [the Confluence page: local developer testing](https://iag.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/CLA/pages/1406403547).

## Prerequisites

To use the GENI API, you need to have the following prerequisites.

### Authentication

MyAccess role required:

- `lumi-prod-{domain}-api-invoker`
- `lumi-prod-{domain}-{role}`

### GCLoud

You need to have the Google Cloud SDK installed and configured on your local machine. [You can download it from google](https://cloud.google.com/sdk/docs/install).

As we use a certificate throughout IAG, you will need to install the certificate on your local machine.

Download the certificate using your browser and then run the following command

```bash
gcloud config set core/custom_ca_certs_file iag-ca-bundle.pem
```

## Daily Setup

To authenticate with GCloud, run the following. As an employee, you will need to do this once per day.

```bash
gcloud auth login
```

## Sending Requests

All requests sent to the API need an authentication token identifying the user. A token is generated and added to requests as below, which performs a health check, making sure the service is up and running.

```bash
curl -X GET https://{domain}-lumi-backend-532613543802.australia-southeast1.run.app/api/healthz \
-H "Authorization: Bearer $(gcloud auth print-identity-token)"
```

> Make sure to replace `{domain}` with the appropriate domain for your environment, such as `genai`, `ambiata`, etc.

## Try it out

> For all endpoints below replace one of the headers with `x-iag-domain: {domain}`. Otherwise, they will not work.

## Bots and their IDs

To work with a bot, often the bot's ID is required. To get a bot's ID, use the `Get Bot Info` endpoint with the url suffix that the bot has for it's chat URL, this will give information about the bot including it's ID.

get.description: Get the current user with roles.

post.description: Post a bot to add to the list of pinned bots.

delete.description: Delete a bot to add to the list of pinned bots.

get.description: Get the list of domains the user belongs to.

parameters.name: offset

schema.description: Offset into file to download. If not specified defaulted to 0.
schema.title: Offset

parameters.description: Offset into file to download. If not specified defaulted to 0.

parameters.name: length

schema.description: Length from offset download. If not specified defaulted to -1 (Take the rest).

parameters.description: Length from offset download. If not specified defaulted to -1 (Take the rest).

get.description: Get all the conversations for the current user.

post.description:
Create a conversation for the current user.

Note that we do not make distinction (permission wise) between a draft bot
version and a normal bot version.

get.description:
Get conversation history with enriched data.

Includes bot version info and question previews.

Gets a specific conversation.
Questions are ordered by created date.
Answer with error is not included.

post.description:
Post a question and get an answer/error back.
Exceptions are handled within each step.

get.description:
Get a question along-with an answer if available.
Answer is None if not available at query time.

Get whether the question has an answer.
Has an answer is True if the question has been answered or the LLM has returned an error.
Return stored prompt optimisation telemetry for a question.
Validate if the user has access to a bot.

Get all bots' information.
For each bot, the latest bot version information is included if existing.

post.description: Create a bot.
Update a bot. Supports only to update the public status for now.

get.description: Get a bot's detailed information.

post.description: Create a bot version for a bot.

get.description: Get all available bot version for a bot.

Get latest available bot version for the bot.

User can select if they want the latest bot version used by the bot, or
their own latest draft bot version.

post.description: Update the latest bot version.

get.description:
Fetches an llm.

Args:
    llm_name (str): The name of the LLM.

Returns:
    LLM: The schema object.

Raises:
    HTTPException: If the LLM is not found.

Retrieves all available LLMs, including decommissioned ones.

Returns:
    LLMList: List of schema objects for all LLMs.

get.description:
Endpoint to return back a dictionary mapping each user email
to a list of their roles eg. {users: {email: [operator, admin]}}.

post.description: Upload a document.

get.description:
Get Confluence pages as content blocks for a question.

This endpoint retrieves all Confluence pages configured in the question's data connectors
and returns them as ContentBlock objects (the same format used internally by LangChain).

Args:
    db_session: The database session
    question_id: The question ID containing Confluence data connector URLs
    current_user: The current user making the request
    expand: The Confluence page expand parameter (default: "body.storage")

Returns:
    List[ContentBlock]: The Confluence page content blocks

schema.title: Response Get Confluence Page Api Data Connectors Confluence Page Get

AllUserGroups.title: AllUserGroups
AllUserGroups.description: Mapping of user emails to their list of roles.

AudioContentBlock.title: AudioContentBlock
AudioContentBlock.description:
Audio data.

!!! note "Factory function"
    `create_audio_block` may also be used as a factory to create an
    `AudioContentBlock`. Benefits include:
    * Automatic ID generation (when not provided)
    * Required arguments strictly validated at creation time

BaseAsyncFileParser.title: BaseAsyncFileParser
BaseAsyncFileParser.description:
Base class for building async file parsers.

This by default implements BaseSyncFileParser, instead of BaseFileParser.
Although this may seem counter-intuitive, it is intentional as by default, the
`_parse_async` will act as an async wrapper for `_parse`.

Citation.title: Citation
Citation.description:
Annotation for citing data from a document.

!!! note
    `start`/`end` indices refer to the **response text**,
    not the source text. This means that the indices are relative to the model's
    response, not the original document (as specified in the `url`).

!!! note "Factory function"
    `create_citation` may also be used as a factory to create a `Citation`.
    Benefits include:

    * Automatic ID generation (when not provided)
    * Required arguments strictly validated at creation time

calculated_max_allowed_pages.title: Calculated Max Allowed Pages
calculated_max_allowed_pages.description:
Get the minimum between the configured page limit and the system max limit.
This is to make sure that if the system max limit is lowered,
existing bot versions are still constrained by it.

FileContentBlock.title: FileContentBlock
FileContentBlock.description:
File data that doesn't fit into other multimodal block types.

This block is intended for files that are not images, audio, or plaintext. For
example, it can be used for PDFs, Word documents, etc.

If the file is an image, audio, or plaintext, you should use the corresponding
content block type (e.g., `ImageContentBlock`, `AudioContentBlock`,
`PlainTextContentBlock`).

!!! note "Factory function"
    `create_file_block` may also be used as a factory to create a
    `FileContentBlock`. Benefits include:

    * Automatic ID generation (when not provided)
    * Required arguments strictly validated at creation time

GoogleGeminiFileTokenPolicy.title: GoogleGeminiFileTokenPolicy
GoogleGeminiFileTokenPolicy.description:
Send a whole file (any mimetype) to Gemini for token counting.

Allow providing a google-genai client via `_client`.
If not provided, with use environment configuration:
    export GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT=YOUR_PROJECT_ID
    export GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION=YOUR_LOCATION
    export GOOGLE_GENAI_USE_VERTEXAI=True

GoogleGeminiStringTokenPolicy.title: GoogleGeminiStringTokenPolicy
GoogleGeminiStringTokenPolicy.description:
Count tokens for a string using Google Gemini.

Allow providing a google-genai client via `_client`.
If not provided, with use environment configuration:
    export GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT=YOUR_PROJECT_ID
    export GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION=YOUR_LOCATION
    export GOOGLE_GENAI_USE_VERTEXAI=True

text_token_policy.description: Policy for counting tokens in text/* files.

image_token_policy.description: Policy for counting tokens in images.

audio_token_policy.description: Policy for counting tokens in audio files.

video_token_policy.description: Policy for counting tokens in video files.

model_name.description: Model that you are counting tokens for.

GoogleGeminiTokenCounter.title: GoogleGeminiTokenCounter
GoogleGeminiTokenCounter.description:
Base token counter for gemini models.

Ensure your environment credentials are set. For Vertex AI integration:
    export GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT=YOUR_PROJECT_ID
    export GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION=YOUR_LOCATION
    export GOOGLE_GENAI_USE_VERTEXAI=True

params.description: Query parameters sent with the request

data.title: Data
data.description: Body data sent with the request

HttpRequestData.title: HttpRequestData
HttpRequestData.description:
HTTP request data.

Attributes
----------
    endpoint: API endpoint accessed.
    method: HTTP method used for the request.
    params: Query parameters sent with the request.
    data: Body data sent with the request.
    headers: Headers sent with the request.

status_code.title: Status Code
status_code.description: HTTP status code returned from the API request.

data.title: Data
data.description: Response body or data from the API request.

duration_ms.title: Duration Ms
duration_ms.description: Duration of the request in milliseconds.

retry_attempts.title: Retry Attempts
retry_attempts.description: List of all failed retry attempts with details (excludes the final attempt).

total_attempts.title: Total Attempts
total_attempts.description: Total number of attempts made (1 = no retries, 2+ = retries occurred).

HttpResponseData.title: HttpResponseData
HttpResponseData.description:
HTTP response data.

Attributes
----------
    status_code: HTTP status code returned.
    data: Response body or data from the API request.
    duration_ms: Duration of the request in milliseconds.

Image data.

InspectValues.description:
Loose dictionary, with explicit typed values (since we know them).

Keys align with the .category.FileValidationCategory StrEnum.

InvalidToolCall.title: InvalidToolCall
InvalidToolCall.description:
Allowance for errors made by LLM.

Here we add an `error` key to surface errors made during generation
(e.g., invalid JSON arguments.)

active.title: Active
active.description: Whether the model is currently able to be used

cost.title: Cost
cost.description: Configuration for cost calculations of the model

metrics.title: Metrics
metrics.description: Performance & capability metrics for this model (optional).

token_counter.title: Token Counter
token_counter.description: Token counter used for this model

max_input_tokens.title: Max Input Tokens
max_input_tokens.description: Max input tokens for this model

max_output_tokens.title: Max Output Tokens
max_output_tokens.description: Max output tokens for this model

multi_file_constraints.title: Multi File Constraints
multi_file_constraints.description: Constraints applied to multiple files when using this model.

file_constraints.title: File Constraints
file_constraints.description: Constraints applied to a single file when using this model.

max_files.title: Max Files
max_files.description: Maximum number of files in a single question.

supported_mime_types.title: Supported Mime Types
supported_mime_types.description: List of mimetypes that can be understood by this LLM.

native_mime_types.title: Native Mime Types
native_mime_types.description: Set of mimetypes that can sent directly to the llm, without conversion.

conversion_mime_types.title: Conversion Mime Types
conversion_mime_types.description: Set of mimetypes that are available to the llm, through prior conversion.

LLMCompatibility.title: LLMCompatibility
LLMCompatibility.description:
Defines the compatibility metadata for an LLM, including supported mimetypes.

This model is used to describe the types of content the LLM can process or generate.

Attributes:
    max_files: (int): Maximum number of files allowed in a single question.
    file_constraints (List[FileConstraint]): A list of file constraints (i.e. size, page count,
        mimetypes) the LLM supports.
    supported_mime_types (List[MimeType]): List of mimetypes that can be understood by this LLM.
    native_mime_types (Set[MimeType]): Set of mimetypes that can sent directly to the llm,
        without conversion.
    conversion_mime_types (Set[MimeType]): Set of mimetypes that are available to the llm,
        through prior conversion.

LLMList.title: LLMList
LLMList.description: Response model for a list of llms.

training_data_cutoff.title: Training Data Cutoff Date
training_data_cutoff.description: Last known date up to which the model's training data extends.

chatbot_arena_overall_ranking.title: Chatbot Arena Overall Performance Ranking
chatbot_arena_overall_ranking.description: Qualitative bucket derived from public Chatbot Arena style benchmarks.

math_ranking.title: Math Ranking
math_ranking.description: Relative performance on math / quantitative reasoning tasks.

instruction_following_ranking.title: Instruction Following Ranking
instruction_following_ranking.description: Ability to follow complex multi-step natural language instructions.

coding_ranking.title: Coding Ranking
coding_ranking.description: Relative performance on software engineering / code generation tasks.

hard_prompts_ranking.title: Hard Prompts Ranking
hard_prompts_ranking.description: Performance on deliberately challenging / adversarial prompts.

long_query_ranking.title: Long Query Ranking
long_query_ranking.description: Effectiveness at handling lengthy / verbose queries within context limits.

response_tokens_per_second_ranking.title: Response Tokens Per Second Ranking
response_tokens_per_second_ranking.description: Relative throughput of generated tokens per second (higher is better).

time_to_first_response_token_ranking.title: Time To First Response Token Ranking
time_to_first_response_token_ranking.description: Relative speed to first token (higher ranking = faster; underlying metric is inverse latency).

LLMMetrics.title: LLMMetrics
LLMMetrics.description: Captures performance & capability metrics for an LLM.

LLMProviderTypes.title: LLMProviderTypes
LLMProviderTypes.description: Enum for all of the supported llm integration providers.

before_size.title: Before Size
before_size.description: The size of the value before the limit is applied.

expected_limit.title: Expected Limit
expected_limit.description: The requested limit (expected size after limiting).

after_size.title: After Size
after_size.description: The size of the value after the limit is applied.

expected_removal.title: Expected Removal
expected_removal.description: Expected number of items to be removed.

actual_limit.title: Actual Limit
actual_limit.description: The effective limit (actual size cap) applied.

actual_removal.title: Actual Removal
actual_removal.description: The actual number of items removed.

limit_delta.title: Limit Delta
limit_delta.description: Difference between actual and expected limits.

LimitMetadata.title: LimitMetadata
LimitMetadata.description:
Metadata describing a limit operation on a collection.

Attributes
----------
before_size:
        The size of the collection before applying the limit.
expected_limit:
        The requested limit (expected size after limiting).
expected_removal:
        The expected number of items to be removed.
after_size:
        The size of the collection after applying the limit.
actual_limit:
        The effective limit (actual size cap) applied.
actual_removal:
        The actual number of items removed.
limit_delta:
        The difference between :pyattr:`actual_limit` and :pyattr:`expected_limit`.

NonStandardContentBlock.title: NonStandardContentBlock
NonStandardContentBlock.description:
Provider-specific content data.

This block contains data for which there is not yet a standard type.

The purpose of this block should be to simply hold a provider-specific payload.
If a provider's non-standard output includes reasoning and tool calls, it should be
the adapter's job to parse that payload and emit the corresponding standard
`ReasoningContentBlock` and `ToolCalls`.

Has no `extras` field, as provider-specific data should be included in the
`value` field.

!!! note "Factory function"
    `create_non_standard_block` may also be used as a factory to create a
    `NonStandardContentBlock`. Benefits include:

    * Automatic ID generation (when not provided)
    * Required arguments strictly validated at creation time

PerformanceRanking.title: PerformanceRanking
PerformanceRanking.description:
Relative qualitative performance buckets sourced from external benchmarks.

The table provided uses labels like 'High', 'Very High', 'Medium', plus
contextual arrows ↑ / ↓ to show direction vs peers. Arrows are *presentation*
only and aren't persisted here; we store the underlying bucket.

PlainTextContentBlock.title: PlainTextContentBlock
PlainTextContentBlock.description:
Plaintext data (e.g., from a `.txt` or `.md` document).

!!! note
    A `PlainTextContentBlock` existed in `langchain-core<1.0.0`. Although the
    name has carried over, the structure has changed significantly. The only shared
    keys between the old and new versions are `type` and `text`, though the
    `type` value has changed from `'text'` to `'text-plain'`.

!!! note
    Title and context are optional fields that may be passed to the model. See
    Anthropic [example](https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/citations#citable-vs-non-citable-content).

!!! note "Factory function"
    `create_plaintext_block` may also be used as a factory to create a
    `PlainTextContentBlock`. Benefits include:

    * Automatic ID generation (when not provided)
    * Required arguments strictly validated at creation time

completion.title: Completion Cost
completion.description: USD per token of the output

summary.title: Summary
summary.description:
Return a concise pricing summary.

Format (always per 1M tokens, 2 decimals):
"$<prompt*1_000_000> / 1M input tokens, $<completion*1_000_000> / 1M output tokens"
Missing values are represented as "n/a".

PromptCompletionLLMCost.title: PromptCompletionLLMCost
PromptCompletionLLMCost.description: Calculates the cost based on linear price per token.

token_threshold.title: Token Threshold
token_threshold.description: Tokens up to this number use `below_threshold_cost` whereas Tokens above this number use above_threshold_cost

below_threshold_cost.title: Below Threshold Cost
below_threshold_cost.description: The cost configuration for tokens below the token threshold.

above_threshold_cost.title: Above Threshold Cost
above_threshold_cost.description: The cost configuration for tokens above the token threshold.

summary.title: Summary
summary.description:
Return a concise pricing summary with threshold brackets.

Re-uses child summaries (which are expressed per 1M tokens):
"≤<threshold>: <below.summary>; >: <above.summary>"

PromptCompletionThresholdLLMCost.title: PromptCompletionThresholdLLMCost
PromptCompletionThresholdLLMCost.description: Calculates costs based on a price per token below and above a threshold.

TokenConstraintEnforcementMode.title: TokenConstraintEnforcementMode
TokenConstraintEnforcementMode.description:
Enforcement behavior for token count presence.

STRICT  - Require counts for all expected token counters; missing -> error.
LOOSE   - Prefer expected counters; if missing, fall back to any provided counts;
          error only if no counts available at all.
OPTIONAL- Same as LOOSE, but if no counts provided, skip validation entirely.

Lower number means "more strict"

total_token_count.title: Total Token Count
total_token_count.description: The sum of all tokens in the distribution.

TokenCountResult.title: TokenCountResult
TokenCountResult.description:
Metadata for token counting.

Whilst seeing the total token count is good useful, in many use cases
you may also need to see the distribution of token counts for complex types.

For example,
- `count_tokens("abcdef") == 6`, the distribution is `6`
- `count_tokens(["abcdef"]) == 6`, the distribution is `[6]`
- `count_tokens(["a", "bc", "def"]) == 6`, the distribution is `[1, 2, 3]`
- `count_tokens(["a", ["bc", "def"]]) == 6`, the distribution is `[1, [2, 3]]`

applies_to.description: The types of files that this constraint applies to.

ToolCall.title: ToolCall
ToolCall.description:
Represents an AI's request to call a tool.

Example:
    ```python
    {"name": "foo", "args": {"a": 1}, "id": "123"}
    ```

    This represents a request to call the tool named "foo" with arguments {"a": 1}
    and an identifier of "123".

!!! note "Factory function"
    `create_tool_call` may also be used as a factory to create a
    `ToolCall`. Benefits include:

    * Automatic ID generation (when not provided)
    * Required arguments strictly validated at creation time

ToolCallChunk.title: ToolCallChunk
ToolCallChunk.description:
A chunk of a tool call (yielded when streaming).

When merging `ToolCallChunks` (e.g., via `AIMessageChunk.__add__`),
all string attributes are concatenated. Chunks are only merged if their
values of `index` are equal and not `None`.

Example:
```python
left_chunks = [ToolCallChunk(name="foo", args='{"a":', index=0)]
right_chunks = [ToolCallChunk(name=None, args="1}", index=0)]

(
    AIMessageChunk(content="", tool_call_chunks=left_chunks)
    + AIMessageChunk(content="", tool_call_chunks=right_chunks)
).tool_call_chunks == [ToolCallChunk(name="foo", args='{"a":1}', index=0)]
```
